full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic water-payload reservoir-with-dispenser for a fixed-wing unmanned aircraft system (UAS) used in autonomous waypoint navigation challenges. Aircraft MTOM is capped at 10.0 kg; the mission requires accurate release of a water payload at a designated target. Reservoir specification: capacity 1.0 L water (1.0 kg); mounted to underside of fuselage via four M4 threaded inserts on a 80 mm x 60 mm rectangular pattern; overall bounding box 200 mm x 80 mm x 80 mm. A single solenoid-actuated 12 mm diameter nozzle port is integral to the reservoir bottom. Material: PA12 (nylon 12) laser-sintered, E = 1.65 GPa, yield = 48 MPa, ultimate = 50 MPa, density = 1010 kg/m^3. Load cases: (R1) Static full-reservoir weight at 1 g: 1.0 kg x 9.81 = 9.81 N downward on the four mounting inserts; max von Mises in mount interface <= yield / 2.0 = 24 MPa. (R2) Aerodynamic pressure during cruise at 20 m/s + 2.5 g pull-up with full reservoir: effective vertical load 2.5 g x 1.0 kg = 24.5 N plus aerodynamic drag 3 N on frontal area; max von Mises <= 24 MPa. (R3) Release dynamics: 50 ms solenoid open causes transient water hammer pressure spike of 2.0 bar (200 kPa) internal; max von Mises <= ultimate / 2.0 = 25 MPa. (R4) Hard landing: 5 g vertical on reservoir with 1 kg water mass = 49 N; max von Mises <= 24 MPa. (R5) Reservoir dry mass <= 120 g. (R6) The reservoir wall when empty (non-pressurized) shall resist external crush of 0.3 bar differential (takeoff aerodynamic) without buckling; linear buckling eigenvalue >= 3.0. Primary verification: structural FEA for R1-R4, linear buckling eigenvalue for R6. Vibration, thermal, fluid (the water hammer is modeled as an equivalent static 2 bar internal pressure, not a CFD problem), radiation explicitly NOT required at this gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242 monolithic reservoir, dispenser interface drawing, and a report listing dry mass in g, max von Mises per load case in MPa, first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic 1 L water-payload reservoir-with-dispenser mounted on the underside of a fixed-wing UAS (MTOM capped at 10 kg) used for autonomous waypoint-drop challenges. Reservoir is PA12 laser-sintered, mounted via four M4 threaded inserts on an 80 x 60 mm rectangular pattern, with a single solenoid-actuated 12 mm nozzle integral to the bottom.

Envelope: bounding box 200 x 80 x 80 mm; internal capacity 1.0 L water (1.0 kg).

Load cases.
- LC1 static 1 g full-reservoir weight (9.81 N) on the four mounting inserts.
- LC2 2.5 g pull-up at 20 m/s cruise with full reservoir, plus 3 N frontal aero drag.
- LC3 release water-hammer transient: 2.0 bar (200 kPa) internal pressure spike modelled as equivalent static.
- LC4 hard landing: 5 g vertical at 1 kg water (49 N).
- LC5 empty-reservoir crush: 0.3 bar external differential during takeoff, linear buckling eigenvalue check.

prompt.title: UAS water-payload reservoir with integrated solenoid dispenser
prompt.brief: 1.0 L PA12 sintered reservoir with integrated solenoid dispenser for MTOM 10 kg UAS. Four static load cases + buckling under external crush.

boundary_conditions.mounting: four M4 insert holes fully fixed
boundary_conditions.loads: body-load on water mass or uniform internal pressure

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Not required at this design gate.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Water-hammer pressure spike pre-reduced to equivalent static internal pressure.

eval_coverage: water hammer as equivalent static pressure
